summary: A Claude Code skill that starts a persistent developer sidebar at session open, loads project module specs into context, checks for unreconciled prior sessions, and launches sidebar.py — a long-running TUI process that listens on a named pipe for hook events and provides real-time spec compliance monitoring, decision capture, conflict detection, and interactive conflict resolution across planning and implementation phases. The launcher supports cross-platform terminal detection (macOS Terminal/iTerm2 and Linux via gnome-terminal with multi-emulator fallback and nohup last resort), requires uv as a system prerequisite, and provides equivalent launcher scripts per platform.
business_rules: On /companion invocation, the skill MUST present all known modules with git-activity-based suggestions before loading any spec, so the developer consciously selects scope.
The skill MUST check for unreconciled sessions before loading specs and offer reconciliation; unreconciled incremental.json files represent captured decisions not yet merged into the canonical spec.
Selected modules and their spec.json contents MUST be written to .companion/state.json before the sidebar starts, so the sidebar inherits the session scope without re-querying the developer.
If a selected module does not yet exist, the skill MUST prompt the developer to confirm creation and provide a description before writing a skeleton spec.json.
The sidebar (sidebar.py) MUST listen exclusively on the named pipe /tmp/companion.pipe for all hook events — it never polls files or registers hooks directly.

On a 'stop' hook event the sidebar MUST call an LLM (claude-haiku) to extract new decisions, rules, tradeoffs, and conflicts from the last 8 transcript messages and persist them to <spec_location>/openspec/changes/<session_id>/incremental.json.

On a 'post_tool_use' event the sidebar MUST map the written file to its owning module, flag boundary crossings (files outside loaded modules), and check file content against the module's non_negotiables via LLM.
On an 'exit_plan_mode' event the sidebar MUST analyze the finalized plan against loaded specs and classify each architectural decision as add, modify, or conflict.

Detected conflicts MUST be surfaced to the developer as interactive prompts with exactly three dispositions: snooze (discard), record (append to conflicts_pending.json), or override (requires a reason, updates spec in place, archives old rule, appends lineage entry).
On 'session_end' the sidebar MUST print a summary panel and exit; incremental captures remain on disk for the next /companion reconciliation cycle.
The sidebar is the sole writer of spec.json files via the override action; all other writes to spec files are prohibited from this module.
LLM calls MUST use claude-haiku, be capped at 60 seconds, and use max_turns=1 with tools disabled to prevent recursive tool use inside the sidebar.

The terminal launcher MUST detect the host platform and select an appropriate terminal emulator — macOS Terminal or iTerm2 on macOS, gnome-terminal (primary) with multi-emulator detection on Linux — and MUST provide a nohup fallback for unrecognized or headless environments.
Each supported platform MUST have a dedicated sidebar launcher script providing functional parity; Linux and macOS launcher scripts are treated as first-class peers.
uv MUST be installed as a system prerequisite before the sidebar process can be started; the skill MUST surface a clear error and installation guidance if uv is absent.
non_negotiables: sidebar.py is the ONLY component in this module permitted to write to spec.json files, and only via an explicit developer override action with a stated reason.
The sidebar MUST NOT write directly to spec files as a side effect of capture or conflict detection — captures go only to openspec/changes/<session_id>/incremental.json.
The sidebar MUST NOT make LLM calls synchronously in the main pipe-reading loop; all LLM work MUST run in daemon threads to avoid blocking the event pipe.
Hook scripts MUST NOT block; the sidebar is the blocking process and hooks are thin relays that write to the pipe only.
The named pipe path /tmp/companion.pipe is the sole inter-process contract between hooks and the sidebar; no other IPC mechanism is permitted.
Skill scripts MUST use ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}-relative paths for cross-skill references, never hardcoded absolute paths.

tradeoffs.decision: LLM extraction runs on every stop event rather than batched at session end
tradeoffs.reason: Real-time capture with lower risk of losing decisions if the session is interrupted
tradeoffs.accepted_cost: Increased API usage per session compared to end-of-session batching

tradeoffs.decision: Captures are written to per-session incremental.json files rather than directly merged into the canonical spec
tradeoffs.reason: The canonical spec is never mutated mid-session by an unreviewed AI extraction
tradeoffs.accepted_cost: A manual reconciliation step is required at the next session start via /anchor:companion

tradeoffs.decision: The sidebar is a separate OS process (new terminal window) rather than running inside the Claude Code process
tradeoffs.reason: The sidebar can maintain a persistent TUI with blocking input for conflict resolution without interfering with Claude's tool execution loop
tradeoffs.accepted_cost: Cross-platform terminal-launcher complexity requiring platform detection logic, per-platform launcher scripts (macOS Terminal/iTerm2, Linux gnome-terminal with multi-emulator fallback, nohup last resort), and ongoing maintenance burden as new platforms or emulators emerge

tradeoffs.decision: OAuth token is fetched via 'claude setup-token' and cached in ~/.anchor/auth.json on first run
tradeoffs.accepted_cost: A one-time interactive setup step is required on first use

tradeoffs.decision: Module path matching uses substring containment rather than exact prefix matching
tradeoffs.reason: Simplicity without requiring normalized absolute paths
tradeoffs.accepted_cost: Potential false-positive module attribution for files with path components that share a name with a module

lineage.what_changed: Add Linux support to Anchor plugin, test the implementation, open PR to original repo, and plan integration approach for TypeScript monorepo project.
key_decisions: Terminal launcher must detect platform and use appropriate terminal emulator; must include fallback for unknown systems
Platform-specific terminal detection logic with fallback case in start_sidebar.sh
Create launch_sidebar.sh as Linux equivalent of macOS launch_sidebar.command file
Add Linux terminal detection to start_sidebar.sh before fallback case
Install uv as prerequisite for sidebar functionality
